author: devs_jun
content:
I was looking at the BAL invalid-test modifiers and noticed that
`modify_nonce` / `modify_balance` / `modify_storage` / `modify_code`
check that the target account exists, but are silent no-ops when the
requested `block_access_index` (or storage slot) isn't found.

The `duplicate_*` helpers already raise `ValueError` on a missing
target, so I'm not sure if the difference is intentional.

None of the current call sites hit this path (they all target entries
that exist), so nothing is broken today — but a silent no-op in an
invalid-fixture modifier could let a bad test slip through unnoticed.
Worth adding a `ValueError` there too, or is the lenient handling
preferred for composability?

author: m0_ox
content: Thanks for the heads up 🫡 . I think it's ideal to have these guardrails in place with some unit tests. I found a few more low-hanging fruit and added some unit tests for this [here](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2710).
